Gordon Beeming OpenAI pricing docs ↗

A Codex field note

What happens when you redeem a Codex usage reset?

I had just finished a task that ran for nearly 30 hours. CodexBar was showing 79% with a flame, and the CLI said I had four usage resets banked. So I tried one.

29h 51mlong-running task completed
79% 🔥CodexBar usage before the reset
4 → 3banked resets after redemption
00

The starting point

A long task finished, but the meter was still hot.

The task landed after 1 day, 5 hours and 51 minutes. Up in the menu bar, CodexBar showed 79% and added a flame because I was running hotter than pace. The CLI also told me four usage limit resets were available.

One very long task later...
01

Open the menu

/usage is where the reset lives.

Running /usage opens the account usage menu. From there, the redeem option sits next to the normal usage view and shows the number of credits currently available.

02

Pick a credit

Each reset has its own expiry time.

I had four full resets banked. Codex listed the expiry for each one, so the sensible choice was the earliest-expiring credit at the top.

Earliest expiry first

03

Redeem it

Codex consumes one credit.

The reset happens in place. A short “Using a reset...” message appears, then Codex confirms the new balance. Four became three.

04

The payoff

Then CodexBar noticed zero.

Once the usage reading hit exactly 0%, CodexBar brought back its celebration. Fireworks filled the screen. Slightly unnecessary? Maybe. Still very satisfying.

How resets work

This was a promotion, not a new timer.

The reset credits were earned during OpenAI's June 2026 pilot. Redeeming one replenishes the current weekly usage limit without changing the normal weekly rollover.

01
Eligible Plus and Pro users received one free reset at launch, with up to three more available through friend referrals during the June 11 to 24 promotion.
02
A referral reset was granted when an eligible recipient sent their first Codex message. These were promotional rewards, not a recurring top-up.
03
A banked rate-limit reset stays available for 30 days after it is granted.
04
As of July 2026, OpenAI has temporarily removed the five-hour limit for Plus, Business, and Pro. The much larger weekly allowance is the limit you need to run down.
05
OpenAI also issues surprise global resets for incidents, milestones, and sometimes just because. To get the most from your own credit, redeem near the weekly limit while its scheduled rollover is still well away.
Read OpenAI's reset explainer See the weekly-only update See the surprise-reset history
